design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore world → encounter random battles → defeat enemies for experience → level up party → progress narrative by defeating story bosses and recruiting party members
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Ryu must rescue his sister from the Dark Dragon Clan while discovering his latent ability to transform into powerful dragons
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Assembling a diverse party of anthropomorphic warriors with unique magical abilities to save the world from an evil empire
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Capcom's first traditional JRPG establishes core series precedent: party-based turn combat with character-specific personal field abilities that solve environmental puzzles, dragon transformation mechanics tied to protagonist identity, and emphasis on character recruitment and party composition as central gameplay loop

difficulty.checkpointing: Battery-backed save system at designated dragon statue locations throughout world
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Party composition flexibility—player can switch any of four active members with reserves at any time, even mid-battle, allowing adaptive strategy to encounters

progression.upgrade_system: Experience-based leveling grants new spells and stat increases; purchasable and found equipment enhances individual character strength
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story progression unlocks access to dangerous areas; recruitable party members (up to 8 total including protagonist) grant new personal field abilities for environmental navigation and puzzle-solving
replayability_hooks: Multiple party member configurations enable different approach strategies
Character-specific personal actions required to find hidden items
Three save slots allow branching exploration paths

world_building_techniques: Character recruitment narrative: each party member hails from distinct clans (Light Dragon, bird-transforming royalty, wolf-man, thief order, fish-man merchant, etc.) with personal motivations
Environmental storytelling: dungeons and towns present story-driven scenarios requiring player engagement with NPCs
Plot-critical boss encounters: Dark Dragon Clan empire serves as antagonistic force driving narrative forward
Protagonist identity-tied mechanic: dragon transformation ability functions as both gameplay and narrative element reflecting Ryu's heritage

boss_design.philosophy: Boss characters represent narrative milestones (conflict with Dark Dragon Clan) and possess hidden mechanics—stronger bosses continue battle after visible health bar depletes, obscuring true remaining health to create uncertainty and tactical depth

faction_indicators: Light Dragon Clan (protagonist's heritage, persecuted minority)
Dark Dragon Clan (militaristic empire seeking world domination)
Kingdom of Windia (monarchy with dragon-transforming royal family)
Ancient thief order (organized faction with supernatural merging abilities)
danger_signals: Dark Dragon Clan's systematic persecution and capture of rival races
Sister's abduction as inciting incident establishing hostile occupation
Revival of mad goddess as existential world-ending threat
environmental_storytelling: Towns and dungeons present evidence of Dark Dragon occupation
Recruited characters arrive as refugees or prisoners from conquered lands
Safe zones marked by dragon statue save points suggest scattered resistance
